F-ITGC-015

Audit event of a non-standard kind

CLEAN IT General Controls Source: SY-F10-006 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Access Change Management IT Operations

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Sensitive changes must be logged and attributable (audit-trail completeness; SOX ITGC).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

A sensitive table lacks change auditing, or a change is not attributable to a known user.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

Without an audit trail, unauthorised changes cannot be detected or investigated.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Enable change auditing on the sensitive tables and ensure every change is attributable to an authenticated user.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

Suggested ownerChief Information Officer
SupportingIT Security Lead · Internal Audit
Monitoring cadenceAnnual reperformance
Phase Timeline Action
1 · Containment
Stop the bleeding		 not applicable No containment required — control is effective on the dimensions tested. Maintain the existing control activities; reperform per the validation cadence below to confirm continued effectiveness.
2 · Root-cause remediation
Fix the underlying issue		 not applicable Enable change auditing on the sensitive tables and ensure every change is attributable to an authenticated user.
3 · Preventive control
Stop it recurring		 not applicable Enforce 90-day password rotation OR (preferred) adopt MFA + SAML SSO; configure automatic deactivation of accounts after 90 days of inactivity; document and operationalise the SoD matrix covering ≥ 15 high-risk pairs; quarterly access-rights attestation by line managers with archived evidence.
4 · Validation & monitoring
Prove it worked		 Annual reperformance Zero dormant active accounts; SoD matrix coverage ≥ 15 high-risk pairs with zero matrix-violation user-rights records; password / MFA compliance ≥ 99% on the active user population.

8 · Management response — to be completed at sign-off

Response Pending — management to indicate Accept · Partially Accept · Reject + rationale
Owner To be assigned
Target remediation date To be agreed
Status Open

9 · Reproduction

SELECT * FROM CUST.AUDITH;
10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-ITGC-016

User references a non-existent function profile

CLEAN IT General Controls Source: SY-F2-001 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Access Change Management IT Operations

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Every foreign reference (third-party, account, parent document) must resolve to an existing master record (COSO Principle 10 — control activities over completeness).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

A record references a master or parent that does not exist in the dataset — an orphan.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

Orphans break reconciliations, hide liabilities/receivables, and indicate a broken create-sequence or an incomplete master file.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Enforce the foreign-key relationship; restore or create the missing master records; investigate the create process.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

Suggested ownerChief Information Officer
SupportingIT Security Lead · Internal Audit
Monitoring cadenceAnnual reperformance
Phase Timeline Action
1 · Containment
Stop the bleeding		 not applicable No containment required — control is effective on the dimensions tested. Maintain the existing control activities; reperform per the validation cadence below to confirm continued effectiveness.
2 · Root-cause remediation
Fix the underlying issue		 not applicable Enforce the foreign-key relationship; restore or create the missing master records; investigate the create process.
3 · Preventive control
Stop it recurring		 not applicable Enforce 90-day password rotation OR (preferred) adopt MFA + SAML SSO; configure automatic deactivation of accounts after 90 days of inactivity; document and operationalise the SoD matrix covering ≥ 15 high-risk pairs; quarterly access-rights attestation by line managers with archived evidence.
4 · Validation & monitoring
Prove it worked		 Annual reperformance Zero dormant active accounts; SoD matrix coverage ≥ 15 high-risk pairs with zero matrix-violation user-rights records; password / MFA compliance ≥ 99% on the active user population.

8 · Management response — to be completed at sign-off

Response Pending — management to indicate Accept · Partially Accept · Reject + rationale
Owner To be assigned
Target remediation date To be agreed

F-ITGC-019

Duplicate invoice number within SINVOICE

CLEAN IT General Controls Source: SY-F7-002 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Access Change Management IT Operations

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Document sequences must be gap-free and duplicate-free (completeness assertion; a primary fraud signal).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

A gap or duplicate appeared in a document number sequence.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

A missing document may be unrecorded (completeness) or deleted (override); a duplicate may be double-counted.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Investigate each gap/duplicate, confirm the missing documents, and lock the numbering counter against manual edit.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-ITGC-024

Shared / generic account logging in from many distinct clients

CLEAN IT General Controls Source: SY-F8-007 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Access Change Management IT Operations

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Incompatible duties must be segregated and access appropriately restricted (COSO Principle 10/11; SOX ITGC).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

A user holds an incompatible combination of rights, or an over-privileged / orphaned account exists.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

A single individual can both initiate and conceal a transaction — the core fraud-enablement condition.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Remove the conflicting access or add a compensating review; disable orphaned/all-access accounts; re-certify access.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

Suggested ownerChief Information Officer
SupportingIT Security Lead · Internal Audit
Monitoring cadenceAnnual reperformance

3 · Preventive control
Stop it recurring		 not applicable Enforce 90-day password rotation OR (preferred) adopt MFA + SAML SSO; configure automatic deactivation of accounts after 90 days of inactivity; document and operationalise the SoD matrix covering ≥ 15 high-risk pairs; quarterly access-rights attestation by line managers with archived evidence.

F-ITGC-025

Active user with no connection login name

CLEAN IT General Controls Source: SY-F9-001 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Access Change Management IT Operations

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Master data must be unique, complete and current (no duplicates, stale or dangling records).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

Duplicate, stale, or incomplete master records were detected.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

Duplicate masters split spend/exposure and enable duplicate payment; stale records inflate the attack surface.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

De-duplicate and merge the masters, archive stale records, and add a duplicate check at creation.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

F-O2C-001

Sales invoice line without its invoice header

CLEAN Order-to-Cash Source: SA-F2-004 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Existence/Occurrence Completeness Cutoff Valuation/Accuracy Presentation

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Every foreign reference (third-party, account, parent document) must resolve to an existing master record (COSO Principle 10 — control activities over completeness).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

A record references a master or parent that does not exist in the dataset — an orphan.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

Orphans break reconciliations, hide liabilities/receivables, and indicate a broken create-sequence or an incomplete master file.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Enforce the foreign-key relationship; restore or create the missing master records; investigate the create process.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

Suggested ownerHead of Commercial
SupportingAR / Credit Manager · Internal Audit
Monitoring cadenceAnnual reperformance
Phase Timeline Action
1 · Containment
Stop the bleeding		 not applicable No containment required — control is effective on the dimensions tested. Maintain the existing control activities; reperform per the validation cadence below to confirm continued effectiveness.
2 · Root-cause remediation
Fix the underlying issue		 not applicable Enforce the foreign-key relationship; restore or create the missing master records; investigate the create process.
3 · Preventive control
Stop it recurring		 not applicable Strengthen the upstream control: enforce credit-limit at SO entry (block over-limit unless explicitly overridden + logged), require VIES validation of VAT-IDs at customer creation, and gate discount approvals above a defined % via the X3 workflow. Quarterly customer-master attestation by Sales managers.
4 · Validation & monitoring
Prove it worked		 Annual reperformance Exception volume reduced by ≥ 50% within 90 days; reached industry-baseline tolerance within 12 months. KPI tracked monthly with trend chart presented to Audit Committee quarterly.

8 · Management response — to be completed at sign-off

Response Pending — management to indicate Accept · Partially Accept · Reject + rationale
Owner To be assigned
Target remediation date To be agreed
Status Open

9 · Reproduction

SELECT * FROM CUST.SINVOICED;
10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-O2C-002

Sales invoice line amount inconsistent with net price times quantity

CLEAN Order-to-Cash Source: SA-F6-001 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Existence/Occurrence Completeness Cutoff Valuation/Accuracy Presentation

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Sub-ledger control accounts (AP/AR/stock/FA) must reconcile to the general ledger.

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

The sub-ledger total does not agree with its GL control account.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

The balance sheet is misstated; an un-investigated difference can hide error or fraud.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Reconcile the control accounts monthly, investigate the difference, and post the correcting entry.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

Suggested ownerHead of Commercial
SupportingAR / Credit Manager · Internal Audit
Monitoring cadenceAnnual reperformance
Phase Timeline Action
1 · Containment
Stop the bleeding		 not applicable No containment required — control is effective on the dimensions tested. Maintain the existing control activities; reperform per the validation cadence below to confirm continued effectiveness.
2 · Root-cause remediation
Fix the underlying issue		 not applicable Reconcile the control accounts monthly, investigate the difference, and post the correcting entry.
3 · Preventive control
Stop it recurring		 not applicable Strengthen the upstream control: enforce credit-limit at SO entry (block over-limit unless explicitly overridden + logged), require VIES validation of VAT-IDs at customer creation, and gate discount approvals above a defined % via the X3 workflow. Quarterly customer-master attestation by Sales managers.
4 · Validation & monitoring
Prove it worked		 Annual reperformance Exception volume reduced by ≥ 50% within 90 days; reached industry-baseline tolerance within 12 months. KPI tracked monthly with trend chart presented to Audit Committee quarterly.

8 · Management response — to be completed at sign-off

Response Pending — management to indicate Accept · Partially Accept · Reject + rationale
Owner To be assigned
Target remediation date To be agreed
Status Open

9 · Reproduction

SELECT * FROM CUST.SINVOICED;
10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-P2P-015

AP invoice line without its invoice header

CLEAN Procure-to-Pay Source: PU-F2-004 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Completeness Cutoff Valuation/Accuracy Rights & Obligations

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Every foreign reference (third-party, account, parent document) must resolve to an existing master record (COSO Principle 10 — control activities over completeness).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

A record references a master or parent that does not exist in the dataset — an orphan.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

Orphans break reconciliations, hide liabilities/receivables, and indicate a broken create-sequence or an incomplete master file.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Enforce the foreign-key relationship; restore or create the missing master records; investigate the create process.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

Suggested ownerHead of Procurement
SupportingAP Manager · Internal Audit
Monitoring cadenceAnnual reperformance
Phase Timeline Action
1 · Containment
Stop the bleeding		 not applicable No containment required — control is effective on the dimensions tested. Maintain the existing control activities; reperform per the validation cadence below to confirm continued effectiveness.
2 · Root-cause remediation
Fix the underlying issue		 not applicable Enforce the foreign-key relationship; restore or create the missing master records; investigate the create process.
3 · Preventive control
Stop it recurring		 not applicable Lock down the upstream control: enforce vendor-onboarding due diligence (VIES VAT-ID lookup, bank-account verification with callback, AML / sanctions screening) at the parameter level. Configure the X3 workflow to require 4-eyes approval on every PO above the materiality threshold and to block self-approval. Enable the duplicate-invoice-number check (INVCTLREF=block).
4 · Validation & monitoring
Prove it worked		 Annual reperformance Exception volume reduced by ≥ 50% within 90 days; reached industry-baseline tolerance within 12 months. KPI tracked monthly with trend chart presented to Audit Committee quarterly.

8 · Management response — to be completed at sign-off

Response Pending — management to indicate Accept · Partially Accept · Reject + rationale
Owner To be assigned
Target remediation date To be agreed
Status Open

9 · Reproduction

SELECT * FROM CUST.PINVOICED;
10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-P2P-016

AP invoice line amount inconsistent with net price times quantity

CLEAN Procure-to-Pay Source: PU-F6-002 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Completeness Cutoff Valuation/Accuracy Rights & Obligations

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Sub-ledger control accounts (AP/AR/stock/FA) must reconcile to the general ledger.

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

The sub-ledger total does not agree with its GL control account.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

The balance sheet is misstated; an un-investigated difference can hide error or fraud.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Reconcile the control accounts monthly, investigate the difference, and post the correcting entry.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

SupportingAP Manager · Internal Audit

3 · Preventive control
Stop it recurring		 not applicable Lock down the upstream control: enforce vendor-onboarding due diligence (VIES VAT-ID lookup, bank-account verification with callback, AML / sanctions screening) at the parameter level. Configure the X3 workflow to require 4-eyes approval on every PO above the materiality threshold and to block self-approval. Enable the duplicate-invoice-number check (INVCTLREF=block).

9 · Reproduction

SELECT * FROM CUST.PINVOICED;
10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-P2P-017

Supplier must have a default currency

CLEAN Procure-to-Pay Source: TP-F1-002 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Completeness Cutoff Valuation/Accuracy Rights & Obligations

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Mandatory fields and valid domain values are required for every record (data-integrity baseline; COSO Principle 13 — relevant, quality information).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

Records created or imported without the required field populated, or carrying a value outside the permitted domain.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

Incomplete or invalid master/transaction data propagates downstream — mis-posting, failed interfaces, and unreliable reporting.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Make the field mandatory at entry, validate the domain, and remediate the existing exceptions.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

SELECT * FROM CUST.PAYMENTH;
10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-P2P-021

Partner master must record a creation user (audit trail)

CLEAN Procure-to-Pay Source: TP-F10-001 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Completeness Cutoff Valuation/Accuracy Rights & Obligations

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Sensitive changes must be logged and attributable (audit-trail completeness; SOX ITGC).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

A sensitive table lacks change auditing, or a change is not attributable to a known user.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

Without an audit trail, unauthorised changes cannot be detected or investigated.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Enable change auditing on the sensitive tables and ensure every change is attributable to an authenticated user.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

9 · Reproduction

SELECT * FROM CUST.PAYMENTH;
10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-P2P-024

Doubtful supplier should not remain enabled for postings

CLEAN Procure-to-Pay Source: TP-F3-001 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Completeness Cutoff Valuation/Accuracy Rights & Obligations

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Document status flags must be internally consistent and progress through valid states (header ↔ line ↔ downstream).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

A status combination arose that the workflow should not permit — e.g. a long-lived temporary entry, or a line with no accounting value.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

Inconsistent status drives wrong inclusion/exclusion in reporting and can mask unposted or stuck transactions.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Reconcile the status flags, finalise or reverse the stuck documents, and add a status-transition control.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

SupportingAP Manager · Internal Audit
Monitoring cadenceAnnual reperformance
Phase Timeline Action
1 · Containment
Stop the bleeding		 not applicable No containment required — control is effective on the dimensions tested. Maintain the existing control activities; reperform per the validation cadence below to confirm continued effectiveness.
2 · Root-cause remediation
Fix the underlying issue		 not applicable Reconcile the status flags, finalise or reverse the stuck documents, and add a status-transition control.
3 · Preventive control
Stop it recurring		 not applicable Lock down the upstream control: enforce vendor-onboarding due diligence (VIES VAT-ID lookup, bank-account verification with callback, AML / sanctions screening) at the parameter level. Configure the X3 workflow to require 4-eyes approval on every PO above the materiality threshold and to block self-approval. Enable the duplicate-invoice-number check (INVCTLREF=block).
4 · Validation & monitoring
Prove it worked		 Annual reperformance Exception volume reduced by ≥ 50% within 90 days; reached industry-baseline tolerance within 12 months. KPI tracked monthly with trend chart presented to Audit Committee quarterly.

8 · Management response — to be completed at sign-off

Response Pending — management to indicate Accept · Partially Accept · Reject + rationale
Owner To be assigned
Target remediation date To be agreed
Status Open

9 · Reproduction

SELECT * FROM CUST.GACCDUDATE;
10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-P2P-028

Partner flagged as customer must have a BPCUSTOMER record

CLEAN Procure-to-Pay Source: TP-F3-005 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Completeness Cutoff Valuation/Accuracy Rights & Obligations

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Document status flags must be internally consistent and progress through valid states (header ↔ line ↔ downstream).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

A status combination arose that the workflow should not permit — e.g. a long-lived temporary entry, or a line with no accounting value.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

Inconsistent status drives wrong inclusion/exclusion in reporting and can mask unposted or stuck transactions.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Reconcile the status flags, finalise or reverse the stuck documents, and add a status-transition control.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

9 · Reproduction

SELECT * FROM CUST.BPARTNER;
10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-P2P-030

No payment to an inactive supplier

CLEAN Procure-to-Pay Source: TP-F8-002 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Completeness Cutoff Valuation/Accuracy Rights & Obligations

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Incompatible duties must be segregated and access appropriately restricted (COSO Principle 10/11; SOX ITGC).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

A user holds an incompatible combination of rights, or an over-privileged / orphaned account exists.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

A single individual can both initiate and conceal a transaction — the core fraud-enablement condition.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Remove the conflicting access or add a compensating review; disable orphaned/all-access accounts; re-certify access.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

SELECT * FROM CUST.PAYMENTD;
10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-P2P-032

Duplicate partner by EU VAT registration number

CLEAN Procure-to-Pay Source: TP-F9-003 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Completeness Cutoff Valuation/Accuracy Rights & Obligations

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Master data must be unique, complete and current (no duplicates, stale or dangling records).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

Duplicate, stale, or incomplete master records were detected.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

Duplicate masters split spend/exposure and enable duplicate payment; stale records inflate the attack surface.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

De-duplicate and merge the masters, archive stale records, and add a duplicate check at creation.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

9 · Reproduction

SELECT * FROM CUST.BPCUSTOMER;
10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-R2R-012

Reversal entry must carry a pointer to its original document

CLEAN Record-to-Report Source: GL-F10-001 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Existence Completeness Accuracy Cutoff Classification Presentation

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Sensitive changes must be logged and attributable (audit-trail completeness; SOX ITGC).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

A sensitive table lacks change auditing, or a change is not attributable to a known user.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

Without an audit trail, unauthorised changes cannot be detected or investigated.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Enable change auditing on the sensitive tables and ensure every change is attributable to an authenticated user.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

Suggested ownerGroup Controller / CFO
SupportingFinancial Reporting Manager · Internal Audit
Monitoring cadenceAnnual reperformance
Phase Timeline Action
1 · Containment
Stop the bleeding		 not applicable No containment required — control is effective on the dimensions tested. Maintain the existing control activities; reperform per the validation cadence below to confirm continued effectiveness.
2 · Root-cause remediation
Fix the underlying issue		 not applicable Enable change auditing on the sensitive tables and ensure every change is attributable to an authenticated user.
3 · Preventive control
Stop it recurring		 not applicable Strengthen period-close discipline: configure automatic period-lock at end-of-month + 5 working days; require dual-approval for any period reopen with documented business reason; route every manual JE above materiality through a 4-eyes approval workflow; weekly suspense / clearing account reconciliation with aged > 30-day escalation.
4 · Validation & monitoring
Prove it worked		 Annual reperformance Zero closed-period postings in next cycle; trial balance foots daily; period-close completes within 5 working days of month-end. TB-balance check automated as part of close checklist.

8 · Management response — to be completed at sign-off

Response Pending — management to indicate Accept · Partially Accept · Reject + rationale
Owner To be assigned
Target remediation date To be agreed
Status Open

9 · Reproduction

Without an audit trail, unauthorised changes cannot be detected or investigated.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Enable change auditing on the sensitive tables and ensure every change is attributable to an authenticated user.

9 · Reproduction

SELECT * FROM CUST.GACCENTRY;
10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-R2R-014

GL voucher header must have at least one detail line

CLEAN Record-to-Report Source: GL-F2-005 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Existence Completeness Accuracy Cutoff Classification Presentation

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Every foreign reference (third-party, account, parent document) must resolve to an existing master record (COSO Principle 10 — control activities over completeness).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

A record references a master or parent that does not exist in the dataset — an orphan.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

Orphans break reconciliations, hide liabilities/receivables, and indicate a broken create-sequence or an incomplete master file.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Enforce the foreign-key relationship; restore or create the missing master records; investigate the create process.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

Suggested ownerGroup Controller / CFO

3 · Preventive control
Stop it recurring		 not applicable Strengthen period-close discipline: configure automatic period-lock at end-of-month + 5 working days; require dual-approval for any period reopen with documented business reason; route every manual JE above materiality through a 4-eyes approval workflow; weekly suspense / clearing account reconciliation with aged > 30-day escalation.
4 · Validation & monitoring
Prove it worked		 Annual reperformance Zero closed-period postings in next cycle; trial balance foots daily; period-close completes within 5 working days of month-end. TB-balance check automated as part of close checklist.

9 · Reproduction

SELECT * FROM CUST.GACCENTRYD;
10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-R2R-017

Final (validated) GL entry must have an entry/posting date

CLEAN Record-to-Report Source: GL-F5-003 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Existence Completeness Accuracy Cutoff Classification Presentation

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Postings must fall in an open period and respect date ordering (cut-off assertion; COSO Principle 10).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

An entry was posted to a closed/future period, or its dates are out of order.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

Period results are misstated; prior-period figures change after sign-off.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Lock closed periods, enforce date validation, and reverse/re-post the affected entries in the correct period.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

Suggested ownerGroup Controller / CFO
SupportingFinancial Reporting Manager · Internal Audit
Monitoring cadenceAnnual reperformance
Phase Timeline Action
1 · Containment
Stop the bleeding		 not applicable No containment required — control is effective on the dimensions tested. Maintain the existing control activities; reperform per the validation cadence below to confirm continued effectiveness.
2 · Root-cause remediation
Fix the underlying issue		 not applicable Lock closed periods, enforce date validation, and reverse/re-post the affected entries in the correct period.
3 · Preventive control
Stop it recurring		 not applicable Strengthen period-close discipline: configure automatic period-lock at end-of-month + 5 working days; require dual-approval for any period reopen with documented business reason; route every manual JE above materiality through a 4-eyes approval workflow; weekly suspense / clearing account reconciliation with aged > 30-day escalation.
4 · Validation & monitoring
Prove it worked		 Annual reperformance Zero closed-period postings in next cycle; trial balance foots daily; period-close completes within 5 working days of month-end. TB-balance check automated as part of close checklist.

8 · Management response — to be completed at sign-off

Response Pending — management to indicate Accept · Partially Accept · Reject + rationale
Owner To be assigned
Target remediation date To be agreed
Status Open

9 · Reproduction

SELECT * FROM CUST.GACCENTRY;
10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-R2R-018

Detail-line fiscal year/period must equal the header's

CLEAN Record-to-Report Source: GL-F5-004 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Existence Completeness Accuracy Cutoff Classification Presentation

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Postings must fall in an open period and respect date ordering (cut-off assertion; COSO Principle 10).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

An entry was posted to a closed/future period, or its dates are out of order.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

Period results are misstated; prior-period figures change after sign-off.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Lock closed periods, enforce date validation, and reverse/re-post the affected entries in the correct period.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

SELECT * FROM CUST.PERIOD;
10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-R2R-022

Legal-ledger journal voucher must balance (Σ signed debit = Σ signed credit)

CLEAN Record-to-Report Source: GL-F6-001 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Existence Completeness Accuracy Cutoff Classification Presentation

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Sub-ledger control accounts (AP/AR/stock/FA) must reconcile to the general ledger.

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

The sub-ledger total does not agree with its GL control account.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

The balance sheet is misstated; an un-investigated difference can hide error or fraud.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Reconcile the control accounts monthly, investigate the difference, and post the correcting entry.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

SELECT * FROM CUST.CPTANALIN;
10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-R2R-026

Currency missing ISO code

CLEAN Record-to-Report Source: MD-F1-002 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Existence Completeness Accuracy Cutoff Classification Presentation

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Mandatory fields and valid domain values are required for every record (data-integrity baseline; COSO Principle 13 — relevant, quality information).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

Records created or imported without the required field populated, or carrying a value outside the permitted domain.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

Incomplete or invalid master/transaction data propagates downstream — mis-posting, failed interfaces, and unreliable reporting.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Make the field mandatory at entry, validate the domain, and remediate the existing exceptions.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

4 · Validation & monitoring
Prove it worked		 Annual reperformance Zero closed-period postings in next cycle; trial balance foots daily; period-close completes within 5 working days of month-end. TB-balance check automated as part of close checklist.

8 · Management response — to be completed at sign-off

Response Pending — management to indicate Accept · Partially Accept · Reject + rationale
Owner To be assigned
Target remediation date To be agreed
Status Open

9 · Reproduction

SELECT * FROM CUST.TABUNIT;
10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-R2R-031

Active business partner missing name

CLEAN Record-to-Report Source: MD-F1-008 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Existence Completeness Accuracy Cutoff Classification Presentation

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Mandatory fields and valid domain values are required for every record (data-integrity baseline; COSO Principle 13 — relevant, quality information).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

Records created or imported without the required field populated, or carrying a value outside the permitted domain.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

Incomplete or invalid master/transaction data propagates downstream — mis-posting, failed interfaces, and unreliable reporting.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Make the field mandatory at entry, validate the domain, and remediate the existing exceptions.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

Suggested ownerGroup Controller / CFO
SupportingFinancial Reporting Manager · Internal Audit
Monitoring cadenceAnnual reperformance
Phase Timeline Action
1 · Containment
Stop the bleeding		 not applicable No containment required — control is effective on the dimensions tested. Maintain the existing control activities; reperform per the validation cadence below to confirm continued effectiveness.
2 · Root-cause remediation
Fix the underlying issue		 not applicable Make the field mandatory at entry, validate the domain, and remediate the existing exceptions.
3 · Preventive control
Stop it recurring		 not applicable Strengthen period-close discipline: configure automatic period-lock at end-of-month + 5 working days; require dual-approval for any period reopen with documented business reason; route every manual JE above materiality through a 4-eyes approval workflow; weekly suspense / clearing account reconciliation with aged > 30-day escalation.
4 · Validation & monitoring
Prove it worked		 Annual reperformance Zero closed-period postings in next cycle; trial balance foots daily; period-close completes within 5 working days of month-end. TB-balance check automated as part of close checklist.

8 · Management response — to be completed at sign-off

Response Pending — management to indicate Accept · Partially Accept · Reject + rationale
Owner To be assigned
Target remediation date To be agreed
Status Open

10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-R2R-032

VAT rate row referencing a non-existent VAT code (orphan)

CLEAN Record-to-Report Source: MD-F2-003 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Existence Completeness Accuracy Cutoff Classification Presentation

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Every foreign reference (third-party, account, parent document) must resolve to an existing master record (COSO Principle 10 — control activities over completeness).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

A record references a master or parent that does not exist in the dataset — an orphan.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

Orphans break reconciliations, hide liabilities/receivables, and indicate a broken create-sequence or an incomplete master file.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Enforce the foreign-key relationship; restore or create the missing master records; investigate the create process.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

Suggested ownerGroup Controller / CFO
SupportingFinancial Reporting Manager · Internal Audit
Monitoring cadenceAnnual reperformance
Phase Timeline Action
1 · Containment
Stop the bleeding		 not applicable No containment required — control is effective on the dimensions tested. Maintain the existing control activities; reperform per the validation cadence below to confirm continued effectiveness.
2 · Root-cause remediation
Fix the underlying issue		 not applicable Enforce the foreign-key relationship; restore or create the missing master records; investigate the create process.
3 · Preventive control
Stop it recurring		 not applicable Strengthen period-close discipline: configure automatic period-lock at end-of-month + 5 working days; require dual-approval for any period reopen with documented business reason; route every manual JE above materiality through a 4-eyes approval workflow; weekly suspense / clearing account reconciliation with aged > 30-day escalation.
4 · Validation & monitoring
Prove it worked		 Annual reperformance Zero closed-period postings in next cycle; trial balance foots daily; period-close completes within 5 working days of month-end. TB-balance check automated as part of close checklist.

8 · Management response — to be completed at sign-off

Response Pending — management to indicate Accept · Partially Accept · Reject + rationale
Owner To be assigned
Target remediation date To be agreed

10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-R2R-036

Company accounting currency is past its end date

CLEAN Record-to-Report Source: MD-F3-001 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Existence Completeness Accuracy Cutoff Classification Presentation

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Document status flags must be internally consistent and progress through valid states (header ↔ line ↔ downstream).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

A status combination arose that the workflow should not permit — e.g. a long-lived temporary entry, or a line with no accounting value.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

Inconsistent status drives wrong inclusion/exclusion in reporting and can mask unposted or stuck transactions.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Reconcile the status flags, finalise or reverse the stuck documents, and add a status-transition control.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

Suggested ownerGroup Controller / CFO
SupportingFinancial Reporting Manager · Internal Audit
Monitoring cadenceAnnual reperformance

3 · Preventive control
Stop it recurring		 not applicable Strengthen period-close discipline: configure automatic period-lock at end-of-month + 5 working days; require dual-approval for any period reopen with documented business reason; route every manual JE above materiality through a 4-eyes approval workflow; weekly suspense / clearing account reconciliation with aged > 30-day escalation.
4 · Validation & monitoring

SELECT * FROM CUST.COMPANY;
10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-R2R-037

VAT code has rates but none currently effective

CLEAN Record-to-Report Source: MD-F5-001 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Existence Completeness Accuracy Cutoff Classification Presentation

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Postings must fall in an open period and respect date ordering (cut-off assertion; COSO Principle 10).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

An entry was posted to a closed/future period, or its dates are out of order.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

Period results are misstated; prior-period figures change after sign-off.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Lock closed periods, enforce date validation, and reverse/re-post the affected entries in the correct period.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

Suggested ownerGroup Controller / CFO
SupportingFinancial Reporting Manager · Internal Audit
Monitoring cadenceAnnual reperformance
Phase Timeline Action
1 · Containment
Stop the bleeding		 not applicable No containment required — control is effective on the dimensions tested. Maintain the existing control activities; reperform per the validation cadence below to confirm continued effectiveness.
2 · Root-cause remediation
Fix the underlying issue		 not applicable Lock closed periods, enforce date validation, and reverse/re-post the affected entries in the correct period.
3 · Preventive control
Stop it recurring		 not applicable Strengthen period-close discipline: configure automatic period-lock at end-of-month + 5 working days; require dual-approval for any period reopen with documented business reason; route every manual JE above materiality through a 4-eyes approval workflow; weekly suspense / clearing account reconciliation with aged > 30-day escalation.
4 · Validation & monitoring
Prove it worked		 Annual reperformance Zero closed-period postings in next cycle; trial balance foots daily; period-close completes within 5 working days of month-end. TB-balance check automated as part of close checklist.

10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-R2R-039

Duplicate exchange-rate key (type + from + to + effective date)

CLEAN Record-to-Report Source: MD-F7-001 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Existence Completeness Accuracy Cutoff Classification Presentation

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Document sequences must be gap-free and duplicate-free (completeness assertion; a primary fraud signal).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

A gap or duplicate appeared in a document number sequence.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

A missing document may be unrecorded (completeness) or deleted (override); a duplicate may be double-counted.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Investigate each gap/duplicate, confirm the missing documents, and lock the numbering counter against manual edit.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

SELECT * FROM CUST.TABCUR;
10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-R2R-041

Duplicate business partner by name

CLEAN Record-to-Report Source: MD-F9-001 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Existence Completeness Accuracy Cutoff Classification Presentation

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Master data must be unique, complete and current (no duplicates, stale or dangling records).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

Duplicate, stale, or incomplete master records were detected.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

Duplicate masters split spend/exposure and enable duplicate payment; stale records inflate the attack surface.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

De-duplicate and merge the masters, archive stale records, and add a duplicate check at creation.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

Target remediation date To be agreed
Status Open

9 · Reproduction

SELECT * FROM CUST.TABCUR;
10 · Sample evidence — 0 of 0 rows (click to expand)
F-R2R-044

Duplicate company by corporate registration number

CLEAN Record-to-Report Source: MD-F9-004 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk High
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Existence Completeness Accuracy Cutoff Classification Presentation

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Master data must be unique, complete and current (no duplicates, stale or dangling records).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

Duplicate, stale, or incomplete master records were detected.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

Duplicate masters split spend/exposure and enable duplicate payment; stale records inflate the attack surface.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

De-duplicate and merge the masters, archive stale records, and add a duplicate check at creation.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

Suggested ownerGroup Controller / CFO
SupportingFinancial Reporting Manager · Internal Audit
Monitoring cadenceAnnual reperformance
Phase Timeline Action
1 · Containment
Stop the bleeding		 not applicable No containment required — control is effective on the dimensions tested. Maintain the existing control activities; reperform per the validation cadence below to confirm continued effectiveness.
2 · Root-cause remediation
Fix the underlying issue		 not applicable De-duplicate and merge the masters, archive stale records, and add a duplicate check at creation.
3 · Preventive control
Stop it recurring		 not applicable Strengthen period-close discipline: configure automatic period-lock at end-of-month + 5 working days; require dual-approval for any period reopen with documented business reason; route every manual JE above materiality through a 4-eyes approval workflow; weekly suspense / clearing account reconciliation with aged > 30-day escalation.
4 · Validation & monitoring
Prove it worked		 Annual reperformance Zero closed-period postings in next cycle; trial balance foots daily; period-close completes within 5 working days of month-end. TB-balance check automated as part of close checklist.

8 · Management response — to be completed at sign-off

Response Pending — management to indicate Accept · Partially Accept · Reject + rationale
Owner To be assigned
Target remediation date To be agreed
Status Open

F-INVEN-001

Lot shelf-life date must be on/after lot creation date

CLEAN Inventory Source: ST-F3-005 0 row(s) tested · sample 0
▾
Inherent risk Medium
Control effectiveness Effective
Residual risk Low
Audit assertions tested Existence Valuation/Accuracy Rights & Obligations

1 · Observation

No exceptions — this control was tested over 100% of the population and passed.

2 · Criteria · what should be (control objective)

Document status flags must be internally consistent and progress through valid states (header ↔ line ↔ downstream).

3 · Cause · why the exception arose

A status combination arose that the workflow should not permit — e.g. a long-lived temporary entry, or a line with no accounting value.

4 · Effect · business and financial impact

Inconsistent status drives wrong inclusion/exclusion in reporting and can mask unposted or stuck transactions.

6 · Auditor recommendation — what should be done

Reconcile the status flags, finalise or reverse the stuck documents, and add a status-transition control.

7 · Remediation plan — structured 4-phase response

Suggested ownerHead of Operations
SupportingInventory Manager · Cost Accounting
Monitoring cadenceAnnual reperformance
Phase Timeline Action
1 · Containment
Stop the bleeding		 not applicable No containment required — control is effective on the dimensions tested. Maintain the existing control activities; reperform per the validation cadence below to confirm continued effectiveness.
2 · Root-cause remediation
Fix the underlying issue		 not applicable Reconcile the status flags, finalise or reverse the stuck documents, and add a status-transition control.
3 · Preventive control
Stop it recurring		 not applicable Configure the X3 parameter to block negative stock at site level; implement weekly ABC cycle-counting with variance investigation; quarterly NRV review and provision for slow-movers ≥ 12 months without issue.
4 · Validation & monitoring
Prove it worked		 Annual reperformance Zero negative-stock balances site-by-site; cycle-count accuracy ≥ 99%; obsolete-inventory NRV provision aligned with policy; inventory-turn metric within industry band.

8 · Management response — to be completed at sign-off

Response Pending — management to indicate Accept · Partially Accept · Reject + rationale
Owner To be assigned
Target remediation date To be agreed
Status Open

7.Standards & frameworks applied

Framework / StandardApplication in this engagement
SOX §404 — Internal Control over Financial ReportingITGC user-access, manual-journal approval, period-close integrity, 3-way match, master-data controls
COSO 2013 — Integrated FrameworkFive-component evaluation of the control environment posture
COBIT 2019DSS05.04 (Identity & access), EDM03.03 (SoD policy), BAI06 (Change management)
ISA 240 — Auditor responsibilities relating to fraudManual journal-entry testing (fraud presumption)
ISA 315 — Identifying and assessing risks of material misstatementAudit-trail integrity, control-environment understanding
ISA 320 — MaterialitySeverity-tiering framework (Section 4)
ISA 530 — Audit samplingSample-size methodology and projection
IFRS 9 — Financial instrumentsExpected-credit-loss provisioning on AR ageing
IFRS 15 — Revenue from contracts with customersRevenue-cutoff testing (O2C)
IAS 1, 2, 16, 21Period-close presentation · Inventory · PPE / disposals · FX revaluation
NIST SP 800-53AC-2 (Account management), AC-6 (Least privilege), IA-5 (Authenticator management)
ISO 27001A.9.2.3 (Privileged access), A.12.4.1 (Event logging)
EU VAT Directive 2006/112/ECCustomer / vendor VAT-ID validation (cross-border supply)
ACFE — Association of Certified Fraud ExaminersVendor-duplication and discount-manipulation indicators
ITIL 4 — Change managementCustomisation register and upgrade-control

A.Methodology — detailed technical approach

A.1 · Control-objective catalogue

The 74 audit views are catalogued in the knowledge-graph table ag_catalog.kb_role_view with role='auditor'. Each row records the qualified view name, the process area, and a free-text purpose. The views are authored by SMEs and locked under change-control; the audit run executes each view verbatim against the live MSSQL RPS01 database.

A.2 · Interpretation library

Every view is paired with a deterministic interpretation rule in scripts/mvp5/audit_findings_rules.py. Rules encode: severity logic (threshold-based, parameterised over population and aggregate metrics), control criteria (citation to SOX / IFRS / COBIT / ISO clauses), likely-cause hypotheses, business effect, and concrete remediation recommendations. The rule source is committed to the project repository and is auditor-reviewable.

A.3 · Sample selection (ISA 530)

For each view we capture the full-population row count and the top-N rows (default N=200) as sampled evidence. For routine populations with N > 250, a sample of 25 items supports substantive testing with acceptable residual risk. For populations the auditor classifies as high-risk (manual journals, closed-period postings, vendor duplicates) the report recommends 100% review.

A.4 · Legal-entity drill-down enrichment

For nine high-value findings (V_P2P_PO_GLOBAL, V_P2P_APINV_CUTOFF, V_O2C_SI_CUTOFF, V_P2P_3WAY_MATCH, V_P2P_VENDOR_DUPLICATES, V_O2C_PRICE_OVERRIDES, V_R2R_AR_AGEING, V_R2R_MANUAL_JOURNALS, V_R2R_CLOSED_PERIOD_POSTINGS) the audit additionally executes a full-population legal-entity aggregation against the underlying RPS01 tables. This produces the company-specific exposure tables and top-N offender lists shown in the Detailed Analysis panel of each finding.

A.5 · Reproducibility

Every finding's Reproduction block contains the literal SQL statement the auditor can paste into MSSQL Management Studio to reperform the test. The Provenance footer of each Analysis panel records the source columns used. The full finding dataset is exported alongside the report as findings.json.

B.Glossary & acronyms

B.1 · Audit assertions (ISA 315 §A124)

Existence / Occurrence
Recorded assets, liabilities, or transactions actually existed or occurred during the period.
Completeness
All transactions and events that should have been recorded are recorded.
Accuracy / Valuation
Amounts and data are recorded at the right amount and any resulting valuation adjustments are recorded appropriately.
Cutoff
Transactions and events are recorded in the correct accounting period.
Classification
Transactions and events are recorded in the proper accounts.
Rights & Obligations
The entity holds the rights to assets, and liabilities are the obligations of the entity.
Presentation & Disclosure
Financial information is appropriately presented and described, and disclosures are clearly expressed.

B.2 · Risk-rating terms

Inherent risk
The susceptibility of an assertion to material misstatement before considering related controls.
Control effectiveness
The auditor's evaluation of whether controls are appropriately designed and operating effectively.
Residual risk
The remaining risk after control mitigations are considered.
Performance materiality
Amount set lower than overall materiality to reduce the probability that aggregated uncorrected misstatements exceed overall materiality.
Significant deficiency
A deficiency in internal control over financial reporting that is less severe than a material weakness, yet important enough to merit attention.
Material weakness
A deficiency, or combination of deficiencies, such that there is a reasonable possibility that a material misstatement of the financial statements will not be prevented or detected on a timely basis.

B.3 · Acronyms

P2P / O2C / R2R
Procure-to-Pay / Order-to-Cash / Record-to-Report — major end-to-end financial process cycles.
ITGC
IT General Controls — controls over the IT environment supporting application controls (logical access, change management, IT operations).
SoD
Segregation of Duties — the assignment of incompatible activities to different individuals.
3WM
Three-Way Match — reconciliation of Purchase Order ↔ Goods Receipt ↔ Vendor Invoice.
SOX 404
Sarbanes-Oxley Act §404 — Management's annual attestation on the effectiveness of internal control over financial reporting.
ECL
Expected Credit Loss (IFRS 9) — provisioning model for financial assets at amortised cost.
AUDITH / AUDITL
Sage X3 native audit-trail tables for header-level and line-level change capture, respectively.
ESPION
Sage X3 login-audit module capturing user authentication events.

C.Distribution & sign-off

C.1 · Authorised distribution

RecipientRoleAction expected
Audit Committee ChairGovernance oversightReview opinion · agree remediation plan
Chief Financial OfficerExecutive accountableConfirm management responses by sign-off date
Internal Audit DirectorEngagement ownerTrack remediation through follow-up cycle
External AuditorStatutory auditorReference under ISA 610 use of internal-audit work
Process owners (P2P, O2C, R2R, IT)Operational accountabilityAuthor management responses · own remediation

C.2 · Document control

AttributeValue
Document IDAUDIT-RPS01-X3-2026-0609
Version1.0 (initial issue)
ClassificationCONFIDENTIAL — INTERNAL USE ONLY
Retention7 years (statutory minimum)
Issue dateJune 09, 2026
Generated byscripts/mvp5/seed_audit_report_v2.py · ruleset audit_findings_rules.py · enrichment audit_enrichment.py

D.Reference data & population evidence 0 datasets · not control tests

The datasets in this appendix are not control tests with a pass/fail verdict. They are the row-level populations, master-data registers, decoder catalogues, and operational benchmarks that the scored controls in Section 6 sample from and reconcile against. Each card describes what the dataset contains, the controls it supports, and how the auditor uses it during fieldwork.

Cards in this section carry no Inherent / Control-effectiveness / Residual rating because none applies — there is no exception count to score against. Use these as drill-down evidence when reperforming a scored finding.

No reference datasets match the current filter

Clear the area filter to see all reference datasets.
